title: Man Ray: When Objects Dream - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: American artist Man Ray (1890–1976) was a visionary known for his radical experiments that pushed the limits of photography, painting, sculpture, and film.

title: Superfine: Tailoring Black Style - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: Superfine: Tailoring Black Style presents a cultural and historical examination of Black style over three hundred years through the concept of dandyism.

title: The Roof Garden Commission: Jennie C. Jones, Ensemble - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: For the 2025 Roof Garden Commission, Jennie C. Jones presents Ensemble, acoustic sculptures exploring sound, form, and the continuity of art history.

title: Recasting the Past: The Art of Chinese Bronzes, 1100–1900 - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: This exhibit reexamines 12th–19th century Chinese bronzes, once seen as imitations, revealing their unique artistry through 200 works from global collections.

title: Allegory and Abstraction: Selections from the Department of Drawings and Prints - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: The exhibition highlights how artists like Matisse, Bourgeois, and others used allegory and abstraction to convey emotion, narrative, and layered meaning.

title: The Genesis Facade Commission: Jeffrey Gibson, The Animal That Therefore I Am - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: Acclaimed artist Jeffrey Gibson (born 1972) invites reflection on the interconnected relationships between all living beings and the environment.

title: Witnessing Humanity: The Art of John Wilson - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: The exhibition features over 100 artworks made over the course of Wilson’s career, many of which have not been shown before.

title: The Magic of Marbling: The Art of Karli Frigge - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: Karli Frigge (b. 1943), a leading paper marbler, created innovative works (1960–2000). This exhibit showcases her sample books, guides, and publications.

title: P. S. Art 2025: Celebrating the Creative Spirit of New York City Kids - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: P. S. Art is an annual celebration of the artistic achievement of remarkable New York City Public School students and their dedicated art teachers.

title: Baseball Cards from the Collection of Jefferson R. Burdick - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: The Jefferson R. Burdick collection at The Met features 100+ historic baseball cards from the 19th century to the 1950s, showcasing legends and varied styles.

title: Casa Susanna - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: Photographs and publications from a 1960s cross-dressing community that met in NYC and the Catskills, created by and for its members.

title: The Magical City: George Morrison’s New York - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: This exhibition showcases 25 key works by Morrison, inspired by New York, highlighting his Horizon series and role in 1940s–50s Abstract Expressionism.

title: Emily Sargent: Portrait of a Family - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: Rediscovered watercolors reveal Emily Sargent’s unique style, shown with works by brother John Singer Sargent and mother Mary, highlighting their creative bond.

title: Making It Modern: European Ceramics from the Martin Eidelberg Collection - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: This exhibition celebrates Dr. Martin Eidelberg’s gift to The Met of approximately 80 ceramic works dating from the 1880s to 1910s.

title: Iba Ndiaye: Between Latitude and Longitude - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: This exhibit highlights Senegalese Modernist Iba Ndiaye’s key work, Tabaski III, now part of The Met’s African Art collection.

title: Lorna Simpson: Source Notes - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: Lorna Simpson: Source Notes highlights 30+ works from the last decade, exploring race, gender, and identity, including pieces from Venice Biennale 2015.

title: Celebrating the Year of the Snake - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: Celebrating the Year of the Snake showcases Chinese art from 3,000 years ago to the 18th century, highlighting the snake’s cultural and symbolic roles.

title: Colorful Korea: The Lea R. Sneider Collection - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: Presenting 100 works from the 5th century to today, highlighting Korean art’s auspicious symbols in paintings, ceramics, textiles, and ritual objects.

title: Afterlives: Contemporary Art in the Byzantine Crypt - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: This exhibit brings together modern-day works that reckon with death and visualize the afterlife with Byzantine Egyptian funerary art and artifacts.

title: The Jousting Armor of Philip I of Castile - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: This special installation showcases a jousting armor of Philip I of Castile (1478–1506), on loan from the Imperial Armoury, Kunsthistorisches Museum Vienna.

title: Ganesha: Lord of New Beginnings - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: Ganesha, Hindu god of new beginnings and remover of obstacles, is shown in 24 works spanning centuries across India, Himalayas, and Southeast Asia.

title: A Passion for Jade: The Bishop Collection - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: Over 100 jade and hardstone carvings from the Heber Bishop collection showcase Qing and Mughal artistry, tools, and jade-carving techniques in this exhibit.

title: Embracing Color: Enamel in Chinese Decorative Arts, 1300–1900 - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: This exhibit showcases the aesthetic, technical, and cultural achievements of Chinese enamel wares during the transformative Ming and Qing dynasties.

title: Arts of Oceania - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: Features monumental art from New Guinea, Pacific islands, Australia, and Island Southeast Asia—regions united by shared Indigenous ancestry.

title: Arts of the Ancient Americas - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: Following a multiyear renovation, the new installation reintroduces visitors to the Museum’s collection of ancestral arts of the Americas, representing the artistic legacy of Indigenous artists from across North, Central, and South America and the Caribbean prior to 1600 CE.

title: Arts of Africa - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: Following renovation, 500 sub-Saharan African works highlight major movements—one-fourth on view for the first time—spanning the Middle Ages to today.

title: Defensive Display: Shields from The Met Collection - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: This exhibit explores shields as defense and visual display, examining their style, construction, and how shape reflects regional and cultural origins.

title: The American Wing at 100 - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: Newly interpreted galleries span three floors, linking the Wing’s holdings from the 17th to 20th century and highlighting relevant, meaningful stories.

title: The Calculated Curve: Eighteenth-Century American Furniture - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: The 2024 reinstallation of The Met’s Wang Galleries highlights American furniture (1720–1770), showcasing key collections and its design, materials, and form.

title: Cycladic Art - The Metropolitan Museum of Art
description: The presentation offers an extraordinary opportunity to closely examine a large body of little known Cycladic works that have been repatriated to Greece.
